text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is easy to use and maintain. First, use clear and logical endpoints that represent resources, such as `/users` or `/orders`. Each endpoint should correspond to a specific action, like creating or retrieving data. Status codes are equally important; for example, return 200 for success, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Always include proper documentation to explain how to use the API, including examples and error handling. Finally, use versioning, such as `/v1/users`, to avoid breaking changes when you update the API over time. This keeps your API reliable for all users.
